name: STM32F103C8
description: STM32F103C8T6 ARM Cortex-M3 32-bit MCU, 72MHz, 64KB Flash, 20KB SRAM, LQFP-48

absolute_max.input_voltage: VDD + 0.3V on 5V-tolerant I/O, VDD + 4V on non-tolerant I/O

pins.name: VBAT
pins.description: Backup domain power supply (1.8–3.6V). Powers RTC, backup registers, and LSE oscillator. Can be connected to VDD if no battery backup is needed.
pins.requirements: Add 100nF decoupling cap to GND. Connect to VDD if no battery backup.

pins.name: PC13
pins.description: Port C bit 13. General-purpose I/O. On Blue Pill board this drives the onboard LED.
pins.requirements: General GPIO. Max 3 MHz output speed and 2 mA sink on PC13–PC15 when VBAT is used.

pins.name: PC14/OSC32_IN
pins.description: Port C bit 14, LSE oscillator input. When LSE enabled (RCC_BDCR.LSEON), this pin is the 32.768 kHz crystal input for RTC.
pins.requirements: For RTC: connect 32.768 kHz crystal between PC14/OSC32_IN and PC15/OSC32_OUT with 6pF load caps. Otherwise use as GPIO.

pins.name: PC15/OSC32_OUT
pins.description: Port C bit 15, LSE oscillator output. Crystal output for 32.768 kHz RTC oscillator.
pins.requirements: See PC14/OSC32_IN. Add 6pF load cap to GND when using LSE crystal.

pins.name: PD0/OSC_IN
pins.description: Port D bit 0, HSE oscillator input. When HSE enabled, connects to 8 MHz crystal or external clock source. Remapped from PA: default function on this package.
pins.requirements: For 8 MHz crystal: connect crystal between PD0 (OSC_IN) and PD1 (OSC_OUT) with 20pF load caps. For external clock: drive directly.

pins.name: PD1/OSC_OUT
pins.description: Port D bit 1, HSE oscillator output. Crystal output for HSE oscillator.
pins.requirements: See PD0/OSC_IN. Add 20pF load cap to GND.

pins.name: NRST
pins.description: System reset input (active low). Internal 40k pull-up. Schmitt trigger input. Drives a reset pulse on the internal reset network.
pins.requirements: Add 100nF cap to GND for noise filtering. Optional 10k external pull-up to VDD for additional noise immunity. Drive low to reset. External reset button: connect between NRST and GND with 100nF bypass.

pins.name: VSSA
pins.description: Analog ground reference for ADC. Must be connected to GND plane.
pins.requirements: Connect to PCB ground plane directly. Should be at same potential as VSS.

pins.name: VDDA
pins.description: Analog supply for ADC, reset, and internal voltage reference (2.0–3.6V). Must be connected even if ADC is not used.
pins.requirements: MUST be connected. Add 1µF + 10nF decoupling caps to VSSA. For best ADC accuracy use LC filter from VDD.

pins.name: PA0/WKUP/USART2_CTS/ADC12_IN0/TIM2_CH1_ETR
pins.description: Port A bit 0. Wakeup from standby. USART2 CTS flow control. ADC1/2 channel 0. Timer2 channel 1 / ETR.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA1/USART2_RTS/ADC12_IN1/TIM2_CH2
pins.description: Port A bit 1. USART2 RTS flow control. ADC1/2 channel 1. Timer2 channel 2.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA2/USART2_TX/ADC12_IN2/TIM2_CH3
pins.description: Port A bit 2. USART2 TX. ADC1/2 channel 2. Timer2 channel 3.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA3/USART2_RX/ADC12_IN3/TIM2_CH4
pins.description: Port A bit 3. USART2 RX. ADC1/2 channel 3. Timer2 channel 4.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA4/SPI1_NSS/USART2_CK/ADC12_IN4
pins.description: Port A bit 4. SPI1 chip select (NSS). USART2 clock. ADC1/2 channel 4.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. NOT 5V-tolerant.

pins.name: PA5/SPI1_SCK/ADC12_IN5
pins.description: Port A bit 5. SPI1 clock. ADC1/2 channel 5.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. NOT 5V-tolerant.

pins.name: PA6/SPI1_MISO/ADC12_IN6/TIM3_CH1
pins.description: Port A bit 6. SPI1 MISO. ADC1/2 channel 6. Timer3 channel 1.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. NOT 5V-tolerant.

pins.name: PA7/SPI1_MOSI/ADC12_IN7/TIM3_CH2
pins.description: Port A bit 7. SPI1 MOSI. ADC1/2 channel 7. Timer3 channel 2.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. NOT 5V-tolerant.

pins.name: PB0/ADC12_IN8/TIM3_CH3
pins.description: Port B bit 0. ADC1/2 channel 8. Timer3 channel 3.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB1/ADC12_IN9/TIM3_CH4
pins.description: Port B bit 1. ADC1/2 channel 9. Timer3 channel 4.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB2/BOOT1
pins.description: Port B bit 2. Also serves as BOOT1 configuration input sampled at reset. Together with BOOT0 selects boot mode.
pins.requirements: Tie to GND (BOOT1=0) for normal flash boot. See BOOT0 (pin 44) configuration table.

pins.name: PB10/I2C2_SCL/USART3_TX
pins.description: Port B bit 10. I2C2 clock. USART3 TX.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to VDD. Open-drain — do not use internal pull-up for I2C. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB11/I2C2_SDA/USART3_RX
pins.description: Port B bit 11. I2C2 data. USART3 RX.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to VDD. Open-drain — do not use internal pull-up for I2C. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: VSS1
pins.description: Digital ground. Return path for VDD1 (pin 24).
pins.requirements: Connect to PCB ground plane.

pins.name: VDD1
pins.description: Digital supply voltage (2.0–3.6V). Powers digital logic core.
pins.requirements: Add 100nF ceramic decoupling cap to VSS close to pin.

pins.name: PB12/SPI2_NSS/I2C2_SMBAI/USART3_CK/TIM1_BKIN
pins.description: Port B bit 12. SPI2 NSS. I2C2 SMBus alert. USART3 clock. Timer1 break input.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB13/SPI2_SCK/USART3_CTS/TIM1_CH1N
pins.description: Port B bit 13. SPI2 clock. USART3 CTS. Timer1 channel 1 complementary output.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB14/SPI2_MISO/TIM1_CH2N
pins.description: Port B bit 14. SPI2 MISO. Timer1 channel 2 complementary output.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB15/SPI2_MOSI/TIM1_CH3N
pins.description: Port B bit 15. SPI2 MOSI. Timer1 channel 3 complementary output.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA8/USART1_CK/TIM1_CH1/MCO
pins.description: Port A bit 8. USART1 clock. Timer1 channel 1. MCO (master clock output — outputs system clock, HSI, HSE, or PLL/2).
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA9/USART1_TX/TIM1_CH2
pins.description: Port A bit 9. USART1 TX. Timer1 channel 2.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA10/USART1_RX/TIM1_CH3
pins.description: Port A bit 10. USART1 RX. Timer1 channel 3.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PA11/USART1_CTS/USBDM/TIM1_CH4
pins.description: Port A bit 11. USART1 CTS. USB D- (full-speed). Timer1 channel 4.
pins.requirements: For USB: connect D- with 22Ω series resistor to USB connector. USB requires 3.3V — do not connect 5V. NOT 5V-tolerant when USB is enabled.

pins.name: PA12/USART1_RTS/USBDP/TIM1_ETR
pins.description: Port A bit 12. USART1 RTS. USB D+ (full-speed). Timer1 ETR.
pins.requirements: For USB: connect D+ with 22Ω series resistor to USB connector. Add 1.5kΩ pull-up to 3.3V on D+ for device enumeration (or use internal PA12 pull-up via software). NOT 5V-tolerant when USB is enabled.

pins.name: PA13/JTMS-SWDIO
pins.description: Port A bit 13. JTAG TMS / SWD data I/O. Used for debug and programming. After reset this pin defaults to SWDIO function.
pins.requirements: Connect to SWD programmer SWDIO. If repurposing as GPIO, disable JTAG/SWD via AFIO_MAPR — note this disables debugging.

pins.name: VSS2
pins.description: Digital ground. Return path for VDD2 (pin 36).
pins.requirements: Connect to PCB ground plane.

pins.name: VDD2
pins.description: Digital supply voltage (2.0–3.6V). Powers digital logic core.
pins.requirements: Add 100nF ceramic decoupling cap to VSS close to pin.

pins.name: PA14/JTCK-SWCLK
pins.description: Port A bit 14. JTAG TCK / SWD clock. Used for debug and programming. After reset defaults to SWCLK function.
pins.requirements: Connect to SWD programmer SWCLK. If repurposing as GPIO, disable JTAG/SWD via AFIO_MAPR.

pins.name: PA15/JTDI
pins.description: Port A bit 15. JTAG TDI. Can be remapped to SPI1_NSS, TIM2_CH1_ETR, or GPIO.
pins.requirements: General GPIO after JTAG disable via AFIO_MAPR (SWJ_CFG). Can be remapped to SPI1 NSS.

pins.name: PB3/JTDO
pins.description: Port B bit 3. JTAG TDO / SWO (trace output). Can be remapped to SPI1_SCK or TIM2_CH2.
pins.requirements: General GPIO after JTAG disable. Can be remapped to SPI1 SCK or SWO for trace.

pins.name: PB4/JNTRST
pins.description: Port B bit 4. JTAG TRST (test reset). Can be remapped to SPI1_MISO or TIM3_CH1.
pins.requirements: General GPIO after JTAG disable via AFIO_MAPR. Can be remapped to SPI1 MISO.

pins.name: PB5/I2C1_SMBA
pins.description: Port B bit 5. I2C1 SMBus alert input. Can be remapped to SPI1_MOSI or TIM3_CH2.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB6/I2C1_SCL/TIM4_CH1
pins.description: Port B bit 6. I2C1 clock. Timer4 channel 1.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to VDD. Open-drain. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB7/I2C1_SDA/TIM4_CH2
pins.description: Port B bit 7. I2C1 data. Timer4 channel 2.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to VDD. Open-drain. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: BOOT0
pins.description: Boot mode selection bit 0. Sampled at reset together with BOOT1 (PB2). Determines boot source: Flash, System memory (bootloader), or SRAM.
pins.requirements: Tie to GND (BOOT0=0) for normal flash boot. To enter system bootloader (for UART programming): pull BOOT0 HIGH and BOOT1 (PB2) LOW then reset. On Blue Pill board this is the BOOT0 jumper.

pins.name: PB8/TIM4_CH3/I2C1_SCL
pins.description: Port B bit 8. Timer4 channel 3. I2C1 clock (remapped).
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: PB9/TIM4_CH4/I2C1_SDA
pins.description: Port B bit 9. Timer4 channel 4. I2C1 data (remapped).
pins.requirements: General GPIO or peripheral use. 5V-tolerant in input mode.

pins.name: VSS3
pins.description: Digital ground. Return path for VDD3 (pin 48).
pins.requirements: Connect to PCB ground plane.

pins.name: VDD3
pins.description: Digital supply voltage (2.0–3.6V). Powers digital logic core.
pins.requirements: Add 100nF ceramic decoupling cap to VSS close to pin.

required_passives.placement: Each VDD pin to adjacent VSS
required_passives.reason: Digital supply decoupling — one per VDD pin, place as close as possible to pin
Bulk VDD supply decoupling — stabilizes supply under load transients
VDDA analog supply decoupling — required for ADC stability
VDDA high-frequency decoupling — paired with 1µF bulk cap
VBAT decoupling — required for stable RTC/backup domain
External NRST pull-up — provides additional noise immunity beyond internal 40k pull-up
NRST RC filter — suppresses reset glitches from power supply noise

application_circuits.name: Minimal STM32F103C8 circuit
application_circuits.description: VDD (3.3V) with 100nF decoupling on each VDD pin. VDDA connected to VDD via 10Ω resistor + 1µF||10nF to VSSA. VBAT to 3.3V with 100nF. NRST: 10kΩ pull-up + 100nF filter cap. BOOT0 tied to GND (normal boot). BOOT1/PB2 tied to GND. HSE: 8 MHz crystal on PD0/PD1 with 20pF load caps. SWD: SWDIO (PA13) + SWCLK (PA14) to programmer.

common_mistakes: VDDA MUST be connected even if ADC is not used — leaving it floating causes brownout reset or unpredictable behavior
VDD supply is 3.3V maximum — do NOT connect 5V directly to VDD pins
PA4–PA7 (SPI1) are NOT 5V-tolerant — do not connect 5V logic without level shifting
PA11/PA12 (USB D-/D+) are not 5V-tolerant when USB peripheral is active — use 3.3V USB
BOOT0 must be tied to GND for normal flash operation — floating BOOT0 causes random boot mode selection
SWD pins PA13/PA14 default to debug function after reset — repurposing them disables debugging permanently until power cycle
JTAG pins PB3/PB4/PA15 are held in JTAG mode after reset — must write AFIO_MAPR to release them as GPIO
I2C buses (PB6/PB7, PB10/PB11) require external 4.7kΩ pull-ups — internal pull-ups too weak for I2C
USB D+ needs 1.5kΩ pull-up to 3.3V for host enumeration — missing pull-up means USB device not detected
HSE crystal needs 20pF load caps on both OSC_IN and OSC_OUT — wrong capacitance causes oscillation failure or frequency error
Maximum GPIO current is 25 mA per pin, 150 mA total — do not directly drive high-current loads
PC13–PC15 and PD0–PD1 have limited drive capability (2 mA, 2 MHz max) when VBAT supply is used
notes: Popular Blue Pill development board uses STM32F103C8T6 in LQFP-48. ST provides STM32CubeMX for pin assignment and code generation. SWD (2-wire: SWDIO+SWCLK) is recommended over JTAG for programming/debug. Internal 8 MHz RC oscillator (HSI) can be used without external crystal. USB requires 48 MHz clock — needs either 8 MHz HSE crystal with PLL×6, or 12 MHz crystal with PLL×4.

passives.description: VDD decoupling, place near each VDD pin
NRST RC filter cap to GND
